prompt: A highly detailed Dadaist mixed-media collage portrait of an androgynous anime boy centered in the composition, shown from the chest up with a calm, slightly melancholic expression and direct gaze. He has messy spiky white-gray hair and pale skin, with one visible eye rendered in a realistic anime style and the face fragmented by torn paper strips, geometric shards, and overlapping newsprint pieces. The entire figure is constructed from layered vintage newspaper clippings, technical diagrams, blueprint grids, distressed typography, cut-paper textures, and photocollage fragments, creating a deconstructed cubist look. Use a muted aged-paper palette of beige, off-white, charcoal black, faded navy blue, gray, and small accents of dark red. The background is a dense wall of torn printed pages and graphic scraps on weathered paper, with exact visible text elements including large “DADA” at the upper left, “anti-kunst” near the upper right, “Die Kunst ist tot / Es lebe / die Kunst” on the left-middle, “ZUFALL / ist / notwendig” on the lower left, oversized “6” and “No.” on the left, fragmented “X”, “UT”, and “frag / ment” on the lower right, and partial year numerals “192” at the top right. Include exactly 3 separate realistic eye cutouts in the collage outside or partially outside the main face, 2 small bee illustrations pinned into the body area, 3 target-like circular graphics on the clothing, 1 small astronaut or suited figure photo at the upper right, 1 mechanical rocket or engine collage element at the upper left, 1 ocean-wave photo strip at the lower right, and multiple circular diagram overlays. The character’s outfit should read as a futuristic high-collared jacket assembled from paper scraps and schematic fragments, with small red label details near the collar and a visible “06” marking on the chest. Composition should be vertically poster-like, tightly packed, asymmetrical, and editorial, with gritty tactile paper edges, glue seams, xerox noise, ink smudges, and strong contrast between organic portrait features and abstract graphic construction. Style: avant-garde Dada, Bauhaus-inflected collage, manga portrait fused with archival print ephemera, ultra-detailed, textured, flat frontal view, no clean digital gradients, no modern glossy finish.
